context.git_context: Version bump from 0.2.0 to 0.5.0 (Alpha → Beta), pytest dependency moved from optional to main dependencies

filtered_findings.title: pytest should not be a runtime dependency
filtered_findings.description: pytest>=7.0.0 is listed in main dependencies array. pytest is a development/testing tool and should NOT be installed for end users of the handoff library. This unnecessarily increases installation size and attack surface for production users.
filtered_findings.why: Runtime dependencies are installed for ALL users. pytest is only needed for running tests (dev/test environment), not for using the handoff library in production. This violates Python packaging best practices.
filtered_findings.recommended_action: Remove pytest from dependencies array (line 28-30). pytest is already in [project.optional-dependencies] test section (line 39), so this is a duplicate.
filtered_findings.evidence: Line 28-30 has pytest in dependencies, but line 39 already has pytest>=7.0 in test optional dependencies

filtered_findings.title: Version inconsistency: pyproject.toml vs __init__.py
filtered_findings.description: pyproject.toml updated to version 0.5.0 but src/handoff/__init__.py still has __version__ = '0.2.0'. For proper SemVer compliance, all version declarations must be updated together.
filtered_findings.why: SemVer requires the version number be the single source of truth. Having mismatched versions violates this principle and can cause confusion in version checking, dependency resolution, and release automation.
filtered_findings.recommended_action: Update src/handoff/__init__.py line 28 from __version__ = '0.2.0' to __version__ = '0.5.0' to match pyproject.toml. Create a version bump checklist for future releases.

filtered_findings.title: Test coverage metric may be misleading
filtered_findings.description: CHANGELOG states '54.5% test ratio (24/44 files)' but actual code coverage is 37%. The 'test ratio' metric (files with tests / total files) is not code coverage (lines executed / total lines).
filtered_findings.why: Claiming 'strong engineering practices' based on file count ratio is misleading. 37% actual code coverage means 63% of code is untested. Users may misinterpret '54.5% test ratio' as code coverage percentage.
filtered_findings.recommended_action: Clarify the metric: '54.5% of files have test coverage' OR replace with actual code coverage from pytest-cov. Consider removing the statistic if not truly representative of quality.
filtered_findings.evidence: CHANGELOG line 15 claims 54.5% but actual coverage is 37% per review context

filtered_findings.title: Duplicate PendingOperation class (neighbor issue)
filtered_findings.description: PendingOperation class is defined in both src/handoff/models.py (lines 20-82) and src/handoff/checkpoint_ops.py (lines 24-224). The checkpoint_ops.py version has more comprehensive validation. While these files weren't changed in this commit, the duplication was discovered during review.
filtered_findings.why: Having two classes with the same name causes confusion and maintenance burden. The models.py version lacks advanced validation. Import ambiguity exists: which version should code use?
filtered_findings.recommended_action: Remove PendingOperation from models.py and update imports to use checkpoint_ops.PendingOperation. Or consolidate into a single location with full functionality. Note: This is a neighbor issue (file not changed) but surfaced during review.
filtered_findings.evidence: grep 'class PendingOperation' finds 2 definitions: models.py:20 and checkpoint_ops.py:24
filtered_findings.note: Filtered out by Layer 1 (delta filter) but included as informational - consider addressing in separate PR

rejection_reasons.QUAL-001: Layer 1 delta filter - __init__.py not in changed files
rejection_reasons.QUAL-002: Layer 1 delta filter - models.py/checkpoint_ops.py not in changed files (flagged as neighbor)
